task_completion.description: Create a parent collection named 'Comparative Constitutional Law'
task_completion.visual_evidence: A folder named 'Comparative Constitutional Law' is visible in the left pane (collections pane) of the Juris-M interface.

task_completion.description: Create subcollections for US, UK, and Canada cases
task_completion.visual_evidence: Three subfolders named 'US Cases', 'UK Cases', and 'Canada Cases' are visible nested under the 'Comparative Constitutional Law' parent collection in the left pane.

task_completion.description: Assign the 12 cases to their corresponding jurisdiction subcollections
task_completion.visual_evidence: When a subcollection (e.g., 'US Cases') is selected in the left pane, the middle pane displays the correct corresponding cases (e.g., Brown v. Board of Education, Miranda v. Arizona) and does not contain cases from other jurisdictions.

task_completion.description: Tag cases with 'United States', 'United Kingdom', or 'Canada'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The tags 'United States', 'United Kingdom', and 'Canada' are visible in the tag selector (bottom left pane) or in the 'Tags' tab of the right pane when specific cases are selected.

task_completion.description: Correct missing court fields for US Supreme Court cases
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the right pane (Info tab), the 'Court' field for Brown v. Board of Education, Miranda v. Arizona, and Gideon v. Wainwright is explicitly set to 'United States Supreme Court'.

task_completion.description: Correct 'Unknown Court' fields for US and UK cases
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the right pane (Info tab), the 'Court' field for New York Times Co. v. Sullivan is corrected to 'United States Supreme Court', and the 'Court' field for R v Brown is corrected to 'House of Lords'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Juris-M graphical user interface to organize collections and edit metadata.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Juris-M application interface being actively interacted with (e.g., right-clicking to create collections, dragging and dropping items).

integrity.description: The agent edited the fields within the Juris-M item pane rather than using external tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: The right-hand info pane in Juris-M shows active text cursors or highlighted text in the 'Court' field, and the 'Tags' tab shows manual tag entry.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands or scripts to modify the underlying SQLite database directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows open showing SQLite commands or Python scripts executing database updates.

privileged_info_for_vlm: US Cases (5): Brown v. Board of Education, Miranda v. Arizona, New York Times Co. v. Sullivan, Obergefell v. Hodges, Gideon v. Wainwright. UK Cases (4): Donoghue v Stevenson, R v Brown, Entick v Carrington, R v Secretary of State for the Home Department ex p Simms. Canada Cases (3): R v Oakes, Vriend v Alberta, Carter v Canada (Attorney General). Missing court fields to update to 'United States Supreme Court': Brown v. Board of Education, Miranda v. Arizona, Gideon v. Wainwright. 'Unknown Court' fields to update: New York Times Co. v. Sullivan (change to 'United States Supreme Court'), R v Brown (change to 'House of Lords').
